In this lesson, I want to show you another option that you have for some more advanced testing. Now, we're inside of Visual Website Optimizer. This is the exact tool that we use here at MarketingFunnelAutomation.com to do the majority of our testing. The only negative of using Visual Website Optimizer for you compared to using the experiments feature inside of Google Analytics is that Visual Website Optimizer is a paid service. You can get started for, I think, about $40 a month. Really, I think they offer a free trial as of right now. If you let your free trial expire, I think they'll make you an offer for a lower version of Visual Website Optimizer for just $19 a month. A great option if you really want to do a little bit more advanced testing rather than some simple split URL testing like in Google Experiments. Now, inside the Visual Website Optimizer dashboard, the first thing that you want to do is click on Create Test. You'll see right out of the gate that there's a few different options. The options that we're going to talk about are these three at the top. The A-B test, the multivariate test, and the split URL test. Let me talk a little bit about the split URL test because this is most similar to the Google Experiments setup. The way this works is you enter a page that you want to test. For example, let's say I want to test this page right here against another page. What I do is we leave the control as the main page that we want to test. This is the current version of your traffic activation page, for example. You put that in here. Then if you've got another URL that you want to test or another version of the traffic activation page, you might put that one, maybe it's version two, you'd put that one right in here. The Google Experiments split testing. We click Add Goals. Now what you do is you choose the type of goal that you want. You can see that this is a lot easier to use than the Google Experiments feature. In this case, you have the option of tracking clicks on a link, visits a web page, submits a form, engages on the page. The easiest one to use is visits a page. You can change this over here to opt-in. Let's say if we were testing a new traffic activation page. Then over here, you would put the URL of your customer activation page. This is again the destination page. This is the goal page. If we were tracking or testing different traffic activation pages, the goal being to get visitors to enter their email address and ultimately get to your customer activation offer page. What we want to do is we put the URL to that page in here as the goal. We click Next. You have the ability to name this test, enter any notes that you want, and you have the ability to, just like before with Google Experiments, you have the ability to change what percentage of traffic do you want to send through this experiment. It's 100% meaning that it'll split. It'll send 50% to the control, 50% to the variation, or you can do less if you want. The cool thing with Visual Website Optimizer is that you can install the Visual Website Optimizer code once on your entire website, and then you never have to install code again. Depending on the theme that you're using on your website or depending on if you're using WordPress, if you're using LeadPay, depending on what you're using, all you have to do is just install the code once. When you first set up your account, Visual Website Optimizer gives you the code and tells you exactly where to put it. It's very, very easy. Again, this feature, the split URL feature is exactly like the Google Experiments, just a little bit easier. The second testing option that I want to quickly tell you about is what's called Multivariate Testing. I don't recommend you use, but I want you to understand what it is. Multivariate Testing is basically where you can test a variety of different elements on a webpage at the same time to see which combination of elements produces the best results. For example, if you wanted to test three different headlines, three different images, three different buttons, and you wanted to see which combination of headlines, images, which headline, image, and button of all of those produces the best results, you'd be able to do that. The downside of Multivariate Testing is that it requires a lot more traffic. The amount of traffic in order to get to a conclusive result, a statistically valid result, the amount of traffic that you need is based on the amount of variations that you have. In the example that I just gave you, let's say you had three headlines, three images, and three different buttons, and you wanted to find which headline, image, and button produced the best result. Keep in mind that there are multiple variations. There's headline one with button one and image one. Then there's headline two with image one and button one. headline three with image one, button one. headline one with image two, button one, and so on. It's a tremendous number of variations. Because of that, it requires a lot more traffic. That's why I don't recommend that early on until you get a lot of traffic, I don't recommend that you use Multivariate Testing. The last option that I want to show you is this A-B test. This is a really cool feature that you don't get in Google experiments. The way this works, it's similar to the split URL test, except the difference is that you don't have to create a second URL. You don't actually have to create a second page. You use Visual Website Optimizer to just make the changes that you want to test. For example, let's click on this. Let's say that I want to test this page right here. You click Create Test and what Visual Website Optimizer does is Visual Website Optimizer will actually bring up the page for you. You can see exactly what it looks like. Then what you can do is you can change any element on this page that you want. If let's say assuming that this was a traffic activation page that you've got set up and this was the headline, let's say, well, if you wanted to change this, you can just click on this text, click Change Text and enter what you want, New, Headline, and then click Done. Now what you've got is you've got the control version over here and you've got the variation version over here. Then you can leave it at that, go on to the next step, and then it asks you for the goal just like I showed you in the split URL version. It's really cool because you have the ability to just make the changes on the fly, make the changes within the page without having to set anything up. The way Visual Website Optimizer works is that it uses something called JavaScript to power this. You don't have to change anything or do anything. As long as you have the Visual Website Optimizer code installed, which again they give you right when you set up your account, Visual Website Optimizer using their JavaScript will make all the changes for you. Then we'll show some people the control and we'll show some people the different variation. So a lot of cool options that you have with Visual Website Optimizer, this is a great tool to utilize once you're generating some money and you start doing more and more and more testing every week.
